archaeology.notes: Repository history and current trust boundaries were reconstructed.

sync.notes: Version 5.5.0, surface, authoritative state, GitHub Release assets, npm provenance, registry integrity, and isolated published-install evidence are synchronized.

prd.notes: Product trust outcomes and time-bounded acceptance criteria are current.

design.notes: The package has no durable UI product surface and detection records no UI frameworks or UI signals.

product.notes: No separate UI product artifact is required for this non-UI package release.

arch.notes: Architecture decisions cover host trust, proof scope, metrics, and self-project truth enforcement.

roadmap.notes: Roadmap provenance is pinned to current source hashes and the published version 5.5.0 release.

stack.notes: The existing dependency-light Node.js stack remains appropriate for the trust hardening work.

repo.notes: Repository structure and public surface claims are synchronized.

yolo-decisions.decision: Use the 15-command core installer profile as the default onboarding surface.
yolo-decisions.reason: It preserves the main lifecycle with lower first-run complexity.

yolo-decisions.decision: Mark design and product artifacts not required.
yolo-decisions.reason: The repository is a non-UI Node.js package with no detected UI signals.

yolo-decisions.decision: Publish 5.3.0 only after merged-main identity and the complete release gate pass.
yolo-decisions.reason: The authorized release transaction requires GitHub and npm publication with provenance and post-publication verification.

yolo-decisions.decision: Publish 5.3.1 only after the Godaudits 2.0 integration and release metadata merged with all required checks green.
yolo-decisions.reason: The patch had to preserve merged-main identity, provenance publication, matching GitHub assets, and published-install verification.

yolo-decisions.decision: Publish 5.4.0 only after the Godplans 1.1 integration and release metadata merge with all required checks green.
yolo-decisions.reason: The feature release must preserve merged-main identity, provenance publication, matching GitHub assets, and published-install verification.
Repository artifacts and durable project facts changed during the run.
